## Specification
Switch Provider from type Provider int (iota) to type Provider string. Add type ModelID string. Update ModelEntry.Name from string to ModelID. Fix enums_test.go. Re-export ModelID.

## Repo
/home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance

## Files Owned
- pkg/ptypes/enums.go — Provider section (lines 290-342): change to type Provider string, update constants to string values, rewrite String/MarshalText/UnmarshalText/IsValid with case-insensitive switch
- pkg/ptypes/models.go — add type ModelID string, change ModelEntry.Name from string to ModelID
- pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go — fix TestProviderRoundTrip (remove int(p) casts), fix TestProviderStringValues (remove int(p)), fix TestEnumOutOfRangeFallback Provider(99) line
- reexports.go — add ModelID type alias

## Detailed Requirements

### Provider string switch (pkg/ptypes/enums.go)
```go
type Provider string

const (
    ProviderAnthropic Provider = "anthropic"
    ProviderGoogle    Provider = "google"
    ProviderOpenAI    Provider = "openai"
    ProviderLocal     Provider = "local"
)
```

String() returns string(p).
MarshalText() returns []byte(p) after IsValid check.
UnmarshalText() normalizes to lowercase, matches against known values.
IsValid() uses switch on strings.ToLower(string(p)) — case-insensitive per UAT amendment.

### ModelID type (pkg/ptypes/models.go)
```go
type ModelID string
```
ModelEntry.Name changes from string to ModelID.

### enums_test.go fixes
- TestProviderRoundTrip: remove int(p) cast in error messages, use %s or %q
- TestProviderStringValues: remove int(c.p) cast  
- TestEnumOutOfRangeFallback: Provider(99) is no longer valid Go (can't convert int to string type). Replace with Provider("unknown_provider").String() → should return "unknown_provider"

### reexports.go
Add: type ModelID = ptypes.ModelID

## Acceptance Criteria
Given Provider("anthropic") when calling String() then returns "anthropic"
Given Provider("ANTHROPIC") when calling IsValid() then returns true (case-insensitive)
Given Provider("unknown") when calling IsValid() then returns false
Given ModelID("claude-opus-4-6") when used in ModelEntry then compiles
Given enums_test.go when all Provider tests run then no int(p) casts, all pass

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] type Provider string compiles
- [ ] Constants use string values matching bestiary
- [ ] IsValid() case-insensitive via strings.ToLower switch
- [ ] type ModelID string added
- [ ] ModelEntry.Name is ModelID
- [ ] ModelID re-exported in reexports.go
- [ ] enums_test.go int(p) and Provider(99) rewritten
- [ ] CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./... passes
- [ ] CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -count=1 ./pkg/ptypes/ passes

## Specification
Update all SQLite queries that use int(provider) to use string provider name lookups via the providers table.

## Repo
/home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance

## Files Owned
- internal/sqlite/db.go — seedMLModels: replace int(m.Provider) with subselect on providers.name
- internal/sqlite/agents.go — RegisterMLAgent: replace int(provider) with subselect; GetMLAgent: JOIN providers, read p.name via ColumnText(3)
- internal/sqlite/db_test.go — update TestRegisterMLAgent, TestRegisterMLAgent_YAMLPermutations to work with string Provider

## Detailed Requirements

### seedMLModels (db.go ~line 258)
Current: `Args: []any{int(m.Provider), m.Name}`
Change to: Use subselect to resolve provider name → id
```sql
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ml_models (provider_id, name)
VALUES ((SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?1), ?2)
```
Args: `[]any{string(m.Provider), string(m.Name)}`

### RegisterMLAgent (agents.go ~line 50)
Current: `Args: []any{int(provider), modelName}`
Change to: Subselect for provider
```sql
SELECT id FROM ml_models
WHERE provider_id = (SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?1) AND name = ?2
```
Args: `[]any{string(provider), string(modelName)}`

### GetMLAgent (agents.go ~line 199)
Current SQL:
```sql
SELECT a.kind_id, m.role_id, ml.id, ml.provider_id, ml.name
FROM agents a JOIN agents_ml m ON a.id = m.agent_id JOIN ml_models ml ON m.model_id = ml.id
WHERE a.id = ?1
```
New SQL (per PROPOSAL-3 fix F3):
```sql
SELECT a.kind_id, m.role_id, ml.id, p.name, ml.name
FROM agents a
JOIN agents_ml m ON a.id = m.agent_id
JOIN ml_models ml ON m.model_id = ml.id
JOIN providers p ON ml.provider_id = p.id
WHERE a.id = ?1
```
Column 3 changes from ColumnInt(3) → ColumnText(3):
`Provider: ptypes.Provider(stmt.ColumnText(3))`

Also update the Model struct construction to use ModelID:
`Name: ptypes.ModelID(stmt.ColumnText(4))`

### RegisterMLAgent return (agents.go ~line 90)
Update Model construction: `Name: ptypes.ModelID(modelName)` (was bare string)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given provider name "anthropic" when seeding ml_models then resolves to integer provider_id via subselect should not cast string to int
Given GetMLAgent with valid agent ID when reading back then Provider field is "anthropic" (string) not "0" should not use ColumnInt for provider
Given RegisterMLAgent with known model when called then subselect resolves provider correctly

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] seedMLModels uses provider name subselect not int cast
- [ ] RegisterMLAgent uses provider name subselect not int cast
- [ ] GetMLAgent JOINs providers, reads p.name via ColumnText(3)
- [ ] Column positions 0-4 preserved in GetMLAgent
- [ ] Model.Name uses ModelID type
- [ ] db_test.go tests pass with string Provider
title: SLICE-4: SQLite query updates for string Provider

## Components Reviewed

### Component: Provider string switch
**Definition shown:** type Provider string with switch-based IsValid()
**Question:** Break uniform int-enum convention for Provider only, or all enums?
**Options:** ACCEPT string Provider only / REVISE all enums to string / REVISE different approach
**User response:** ACCEPT — string Provider only
**User note:** "Shouldn't the model name be string as well?"
**Feedback:** Concern about IsValid edge cases

### Component: ModelID type (NEW — surfaced by user)
**Question:** Should provenance adopt type ModelID string matching bestiary?
**Options:** Add ModelID string type (Recommended) / Keep bare string / Use bestiary.ModelID directly
**User response:** Add ModelID string type (Recommended)

### Component: IsValid normalization
**Question:** Strict exact-match or case-insensitive?
**Options:** Strict exact-match (Recommended) / Case-insensitive normalization
**User response:** Case-insensitive normalization

### Component: Adapter API
**First pass:** REVISE — "Why are we discarding the tests? Also should be RegistryFromBestiary(bestiary.Models())"
**Clarified:** Tests preserved, NewRegistry is additive, DefaultModelRegistry uses bestiary.Models()
**Second pass:** ACCEPT

### Component: SQLite bridging
**Question:** Integer PK + name subselect
**User response:** ACCEPT

### Catch-all
**User response:** All requirements covered

## Final Decision
**ACCEPT with 2 amendments:**
1. Add type ModelID string to ptypes (new requirement from user)
2. Provider.IsValid() and UnmarshalText should be case-insensitive

## Amendments to PROPOSAL-3
These must be incorporated into the ratified plan before implementation.

# Handoff: Architect → Supervisor

## Supervisor Startup
1. Call `Skill(/aura:supervisor)` to load your role instructions
2. Read the RATIFIED PROPOSAL with `bd show providence-mnq`
3. Read the URD with `bd show providence-tul`
4. Read the UAT amendments with `bd show providence-7e7`
5. Every vertical slice MUST have leaf tasks (L1: types, L2: tests, L3: impl)

## Summary

Integrate dayvidpham/bestiary as the model data source for dayvidpham/provenance. Switch Provider from int to string enum, add ModelID string type, create adapter that converts bestiary.ModelInfo → provenance.ModelEntry, make DefaultModelRegistry() use bestiary.Models() as single source of truth.

## Repos
- Provenance: /home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance
- Bestiary: /home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/bestiary/main/

## Slice Dependency Order

```
Slice 1 (bestiary: add LookupModelByProvider + Models + tag v0.1.0)
    │
Slice 0 (provenance: go get bestiary@v0.1.0)
    │
Slice 2 (provenance: Provider string + ModelID type + enums_test fixes)
    │         (parallel with Slice 0 — no bestiary import)
    │
    ├──► Slice 3 (provenance: adapter.go + NewRegistry + DefaultModelRegistry swap)
    │         (needs Slice 0 + Slice 2)
    │
    └──► Slice 4 (provenance: SQLite queries — seedMLModels, RegisterMLAgent, GetMLAgent)
              (needs Slice 2)
              │
              ▼
         Slice 5 (provenance: test updates + new tests)
              (needs all prior)
```

## Key Files

### Bestiary (Slice 1)
- `registry.go` — add LookupModelByProvider, Models

### Provenance
- `pkg/ptypes/enums.go` — Provider string switch, IsValid case-insensitive
- `pkg/ptypes/models.go` — add ModelID type, update ModelEntry.Name
- `models.go` — rename defaultRegistry → inMemoryRegistry, add NewRegistry, update DefaultModelRegistry
- `adapter.go` — NEW: RegistryFromBestiary()
- `options.go` — unchanged
- `provenance.go` — RegisterMLAgent signature: modelName string → ModelID
- `tracker.go` — registry Lookup uses ModelID
- `reexports.go` — re-export ModelID
- `internal/sqlite/db.go` — seedMLModels: provider name subselect
- `internal/sqlite/agents.go` — RegisterMLAgent: provider name subselect, GetMLAgent: JOIN providers
- `internal/testutil/fixtures.go` — TestModels uses ModelID
- `models_test.go` — dynamic counts, Lookup existence checks, Provider edge cases, adapter round-trip
- `demo_test.go` — ModelID constants in RegisterMLAgent calls
- `tracker_test.go` — ModelID constants
- `internal/sqlite/db_test.go` — ModelID constants
- `go.mod` — add bestiary@v0.1.0

## UAT Amendments (from providence-7e7)

1. **Add type ModelID string** — ModelEntry.Name becomes ModelID. RegisterMLAgent modelName param becomes ModelID. Matches bestiary.ModelID.
2. **Case-insensitive IsValid** — Provider.IsValid() and UnmarshalText use strings.EqualFold or strings.ToLower. Provider("Anthropic").IsValid() returns true.

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] type Provider string compiles with all named-constant call sites
- [ ] type ModelID string added, ModelEntry.Name is ModelID
- [ ] RegisterMLAgent signature uses ModelID
- [ ] Provider.IsValid() case-insensitive via switch on strings.ToLower
- [ ] 3 SQLite int(provider) casts replaced with string name lookups
- [ ] enums_test.go int(p) and Provider(99) rewritten
- [ ] models_test.go uses dynamic count, not hardcoded 3
- [ ] GetMLAgent JOINs providers table, reads p.name via ColumnText(3)
- [ ] IsValid() uses switch, not range comparison
- [ ] CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./... passes
- [ ] CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -count=1 ./... passes
- [ ] go vet clean
- [ ] bestiary pinned to v0.1.0 in go.mod
- [ ] No bestiary import in pkg/ptypes/ or internal/
- [ ] RegistryFromBestiary round-trip tested
- [ ] Provider edge case tests (empty, wrong case, unknown)
- [ ] inMemoryRegistry renamed from defaultRegistry
- [ ] DefaultModelRegistry uses bestiary.Models() not StaticModels()
- [ ] NewRegistry([]ModelEntry) exposed publicly

## BDD Acceptance Criteria

Given provenance with default config when OpenSQLite(path) called then ml_models seeded from bestiary.Models() should not use hardcoded model list

Given a caller using ProviderAnthropic constant when passing to RegisterMLAgent then compiles and works identically should not require caller changes

Given bestiary.ModelInfo when converted via RegistryFromBestiary then only Provider, Name (ModelID), DisplayName, Family extracted should not leak bestiary types into ptypes

Given provider name "anthropic" when seeding ml_models then resolves to integer provider_id via subselect should not cast string to int directly

Given Provider("Unknown") when calling IsValid() then returns true (case-insensitive matches "unknown" which is not known → false) should not panic or use range comparison

Given Provider("ANTHROPIC") when calling IsValid() then returns true (case-insensitive match to "anthropic") should not reject valid providers due to case

Given GetMLAgent with valid agent ID when reading back then Provider field is "anthropic" (string) not "0" (int cast) should not use ColumnInt for provider

Given RegisterMLAgent with ModelID("claude-opus-4-6") when called then compiles with typed model name should not accept bare string without ModelID conversion

## Questions and Responses

### Round 1: Provider Type
Q: Should provenance Provider type switch from int enum to string enum to match bestiary, or keep int and bridge at the boundary?
Options: Switch to string (Recommended), Keep int bridge internally, Use bestiary.Provider directly
A: **Switch to string** — type Provider string, matching bestiary. Eliminates bridging. 44 call sites compile unchanged.

### Round 1: ModelEntry Type
Q: Should provenance ModelEntry be replaced by bestiary.ModelInfo, or keep its own slim type and convert at the boundary?
Options: Use bestiary.ModelInfo directly, Keep slim ModelEntry convert (Recommended)
A: **Keep slim ModelEntry, convert at boundary** — ptypes stays zero-dependency leaf package. Adapter converts bestiary.ModelInfo → ModelEntry.

### Round 2: Adapter Location
Q: Where should the bestiary adapter live?
Options: Root package (Recommended), Separate pkg/bestiary/ subpackage
A: **Root package** — RegistryFromBestiary() in root provenance package. Only root imports bestiary.

### Round 2: Default Registry
Q: Should the default registry still use hardcoded defaultModels, or call bestiary.StaticModels()?
Options: Default uses bestiary (Recommended), Default stays hardcoded
A: **Default uses bestiary** — DefaultModelRegistry() calls bestiary.StaticModels() internally. Single source of truth.

### Round 2: Bestiary Changes
Q: Should we add LookupModelByProvider and Models() to bestiary in this epoch or separately?
Options: Add in this epoch (Recommended), Separate task
A: **Add in this epoch** — both repos change in one coordinated push.

### Round 3: Schema
Q: Should providers table switch to text PK or keep integer PK with string lookup?
Options: Text PK (Recommended), Keep integer PK lookup by name
A: **Keep integer PK, lookup by name** — providers(id INTEGER PK, name TEXT UNIQUE). Queries join on name. Preserves existing schema.

### Round 3: Catch-all
Q: Anything else?
Options: Migration concern, Bestiary version pinning, Nothing else
A: **Bestiary version pinning** — pin bestiary to a specific version/tag rather than latest main.

No range comparison — lexicographic ordering is irrelevant.

"44 call sites compile unchanged" replaced with specific accounting:

Fixed — JOIN providers table to get name string:
```sql
SELECT a.id, a.kind_id,
       m.role_id, ml.name, p.name AS provider_name, ml.id AS model_id
FROM agents a
JOIN agents_ml m ON a.id = m.agent_id
JOIN ml_models ml ON m.model_id = ml.id
JOIN providers p ON ml.provider_id = p.id
WHERE a.id = ?
```

## Problem Space

Provenance tracks ML agent lineage using a local model registry with hardcoded model names. Bestiary provides a comprehensive, updatable model catalog sourced from models.dev. The two projects share the same domain (providers, model IDs) but use incompatible types: provenance uses `type Provider int` (iota), bestiary uses `type Provider string`.

**Axes:**
- Cross-repo coordination: bestiary and provenance change in one epoch
- Type bridging: string Provider at Go level, integer FK at SQLite level
- Dependency direction: provenance imports bestiary (not vice versa)

**Has-a / Is-a:**
- Provenance has-a ModelRegistry (interface for model catalog)
- Bestiary is-a model catalog (the canonical data source)
- ModelEntry is-a slim projection of bestiary.ModelInfo (4 of 25 fields)

## Engineering Tradeoffs

| Decision | Options | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Provider type | int enum / string enum / bestiary.Provider directly | string enum | Matches bestiary, zero caller changes (constants keep names), eliminates bridging layer |
| ModelEntry | bestiary.ModelInfo directly / slim 4-field struct | Slim struct | ptypes stays zero-dependency leaf package |
| Adapter location | root package / separate subpackage | Root package | Simpler, discoverable, only one file imports bestiary |
| Default registry | hardcoded / bestiary.StaticModels() | bestiary | Single source of truth, models stay in sync |
| SQLite schema | text PK / integer PK with name lookup | Integer PK + name lookup | Preserves existing schema, no migration needed |

## Implementation Plan

### Slice 1: Bestiary API additions (dayvidpham/bestiary)

Add two functions to bestiary/registry.go:

```go
// LookupModelByProvider finds a model by (provider, name) pair.
func LookupModelByProvider(p Provider, name string) (ModelInfo, bool) {
    for _, m := range staticModels {
        if m.Provider == p && string(m.ID) == name {
            return m, true
        }
    }
    return ModelInfo{}, false
}

// Models returns all known models (static catalog).
func Models() []ModelInfo {
    return StaticModels()
}
```

Tag a release (e.g., v0.1.0) for version pinning.

### Slice 2: Provider string switch (provenance/pkg/ptypes)

Change Provider from int to string in pkg/ptypes/enums.go:

```go
// Before:
type Provider int
const (
    ProviderAnthropic Provider = iota // 0
    ProviderGoogle                    // 1
    ProviderOpenAI                    // 2
    ProviderLocal                     // 3
)

// After:
type Provider string
const (
    ProviderAnthropic Provider = "anthropic"
    ProviderGoogle    Provider = "google"
    ProviderOpenAI    Provider = "openai"
    ProviderLocal     Provider = "local"
)
```

Update methods: String() returns string(p), MarshalText/UnmarshalText adapt, IsValid() checks against known set. Remove providerStrings array (no longer needed).

Update all SQLite queries that cast `int(provider)` to use `string(provider)` or the providers table name lookup:

```sql
-- seedMLModels: resolve provider name to id
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ml_models (provider_id, name)
  VALUES ((SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?), ?)

-- RegisterMLAgent: resolve provider name to id  
SELECT id FROM ml_models
  WHERE provider_id = (SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?1)
  AND name = ?2
```

### Slice 3: Adapter + default registry swap (provenance root)

New file adapter.go:

```go
package provenance

import "github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary"

// RegistryFromBestiary converts bestiary models into a provenance ModelRegistry.
func RegistryFromBestiary(models []bestiary.ModelInfo) ModelRegistry {
    entries := make([]ModelEntry, len(models))
    for i, m := range models {
        entries[i] = ModelEntry{
            Provider:    Provider(m.Provider),
            Name:        string(m.ID),
            DisplayName: m.DisplayName,
            Family:      m.Family,
        }
    }
    return NewRegistry(entries)
}
```

Extract NewRegistry([]ModelEntry) ModelRegistry from the existing defaultRegistry:

```go
// NewRegistry creates a ModelRegistry from a slice of ModelEntry.
func NewRegistry(entries []ModelEntry) ModelRegistry {
    idx := make(map[registryKey]ModelEntry, len(entries))
    for _, m := range entries {
        idx[registryKey{provider: m.Provider, name: m.Name}] = m
    }
    return &defaultRegistry{entries: entries, index: idx}
}
```

Update DefaultModelRegistry():

```go
func DefaultModelRegistry() ModelRegistry {
    return RegistryFromBestiary(bestiary.StaticModels())
}
```

Remove hardcoded defaultModels slice.

### Slice 4: SQLite query updates (provenance/internal/sqlite)

Update seedMLModels in db.go — resolve provider by name:

```go
func (db *DB) seedMLModels(models []ptypes.ModelEntry) error {
    for _, m := range models {
        if err := sqlitex.Execute(db.conn,
            `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ml_models (provider_id, name)
             VALUES ((SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?), ?)`,
            &sqlitex.ExecOptions{Args: []any{string(m.Provider), m.Name}},
        ); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("seedMLModels: inserting model (%s, %q): %w",
                m.Provider, m.Name, err)
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

Update RegisterMLAgent in agents.go — resolve provider by name:

```go
// Line 50: change query
`SELECT id FROM ml_models WHERE provider_id = (SELECT id FROM providers WHERE name = ?1) AND name = ?2`
// Line 52: change arg
Args: []any{string(provider), modelName},
```

Update GetMLAgent in agents.go — resolve provider from join:

```go
// The SELECT that reads back Provider needs to join providers table
// to get name string instead of integer id
```

### Slice 5: Test updates (provenance)

- Update testutil.TestModels() to use string Provider values
- Update all test assertions that compare Provider values
- Add adapter_test.go: TestRegistryFromBestiary with round-trip verification
- Add test for NewRegistry with custom entries
- Pin bestiary version in go.mod

### Slice 6: go.mod + version pin

```bash
cd /home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance
go get github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary@v0.1.0
```

## Dependency Order

```
Slice 1 (bestiary API) ─── must complete first, tag release
    │
    ▼
Slice 2 (Provider string) ─── no bestiary dependency, can parallel with Slice 1
    │
    ├──► Slice 3 (adapter) ─── needs string Provider + bestiary import
    │
    └──► Slice 4 (SQLite queries) ─── needs string Provider
              │
              ▼
         Slice 5 (tests) ─── needs all prior slices
              │
              ▼
         Slice 6 (go.mod pin) ─── final
```

Slices 1 and 2 can run in parallel. Slices 3 and 4 can run in parallel after Slice 2. Slice 5 after 3+4. Slice 6 last.

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] `type Provider string` compiles with all 44 call sites unchanged
- [ ] `CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./...` passes
- [ ] `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -count=1 ./...` passes (all packages)
- [ ] `go vet ./...` clean
- [ ] bestiary.StaticModels() is the default model source
- [ ] RegisterMLAgent validates via registry Lookup before DB
- [ ] SQLite queries resolve provider by name, not int cast
- [ ] No bestiary import in pkg/ptypes/ or internal/
- [ ] bestiary pinned to specific version in go.mod
- [ ] RegistryFromBestiary round-trips correctly

## BDD Acceptance Criteria

**Given** provenance with default config **when** OpenSQLite(path) is called **then** ml_models is seeded from bestiary.StaticModels() **should not** use hardcoded model list

**Given** a caller using ProviderAnthropic constant **when** passing to RegisterMLAgent **then** compiles and works identically to before **should not** require any caller changes

**Given** bestiary.ModelInfo with 25 fields **when** converted via RegistryFromBestiary **then** only Provider, Name, DisplayName, Family are extracted **should not** leak bestiary types into ptypes

**Given** a provider name "anthropic" **when** seeding ml_models **then** resolves to integer provider_id via subselect on providers table **should not** cast string to int directly

**Given** a custom ModelRegistry **when** passed via WithModelRegistry **then** overrides the bestiary default completely **should not** merge with or fall back to default models

```go
package provenance

import (
    "github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/provenance/pkg/ptypes"
)

// RegistryFromBestiary converts bestiary model data into a provenance ModelRegistry.
// Only Provider, Name (as ModelID), DisplayName, and Family are extracted.
// This is the only file that imports bestiary.
func RegistryFromBestiary(models []bestiary.ModelInfo) ptypes.ModelRegistry {
    entries := make([]ptypes.ModelEntry, len(models))
    for i, m := range models {
        entries[i] = ptypes.ModelEntry{
            Provider:    ptypes.Provider(m.Provider),
            Name:        ptypes.ModelID(m.ID),
            DisplayName: m.DisplayName,
            Family:      m.Family,
        }
    }
    return NewRegistry(entries)
}
```

## Changes from PROPOSAL-2

PROPOSAL-2 core architecture is STABLE. This revision fixes 4 specification gaps:

### Fix F3: GetMLAgent SQL — preserve column ordering

The current SQL (agents.go:199):
```sql
SELECT a.kind_id, m.role_id, ml.id, ml.provider_id, ml.name
FROM agents a
JOIN agents_ml m ON a.id = m.agent_id
JOIN ml_models ml ON m.model_id = ml.id
WHERE a.id = ?1
```

Fixed — replace `ml.provider_id` with `p.name`, add providers JOIN. Column positions 0-4 unchanged:
```sql
SELECT a.kind_id, m.role_id, ml.id, p.name, ml.name
FROM agents a
JOIN agents_ml m ON a.id = m.agent_id
JOIN ml_models ml ON m.model_id = ml.id
JOIN providers p ON ml.provider_id = p.id
WHERE a.id = ?1
```

Go read changes — only column 3:
```go
// Before: Provider: ptypes.Provider(stmt.ColumnInt(3))
// After:  Provider: ptypes.Provider(stmt.ColumnText(3))
```

Columns 0 (kind_id), 1 (role_id), 2 (ml.id), 4 (ml.name) are unchanged. No silent data corruption.

Same pattern applies to RegisterMLAgent return path — check if it also reads provider_id and fix identically.

### Fix B2a: TestDefaultModelRegistry_ModelsByProvider

Replace hardcoded counts with existence checks:
```go
func TestDefaultModelRegistry_ModelsByProvider(t *testing.T) {
    reg := provenance.DefaultModelRegistry()

    anthropic := reg.ModelsByProvider(provenance.ProviderAnthropic)
    if len(anthropic) == 0 {
        t.Error("ModelsByProvider(Anthropic) returned empty")
    }
    // Verify at least one known model exists
    found := false
    for _, m := range anthropic {
        if m.Name == "claude-opus-4-6" {
            found = true
            break
        }
    }
    if !found {
        t.Error("ModelsByProvider(Anthropic) missing claude-opus-4-6")
    }

    // Provider with no models in registry should return empty
    local := reg.ModelsByProvider(provenance.ProviderLocal)
    if len(local) != 0 {
        t.Errorf("ModelsByProvider(Local) = %d, want 0", len(local))
    }
}
```

### Fix B2b: TestDefaultRegistry_LookupRejectsBeforeDB

Use a model NOT in bestiary.StaticModels() — `ProviderLocal, "nonexistent-model"`:
```go
func TestDefaultRegistry_LookupRejectsBeforeDB(t *testing.T) {
    tr, err := provenance.OpenMemory()
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("OpenMemory: %v", err)
    }
    defer tr.Close()

    // ProviderLocal with a fake model — not in bestiary catalog
    _, err = tr.RegisterMLAgent("ns", provenance.RoleWorker,
        provenance.ProviderLocal, "nonexistent-model")
    if !errors.Is(err, provenance.ErrNotFound) {
        t.Errorf("got %v, want ErrNotFound", err)
    }
}
```

### Fix B2c: TestDefaultModelRegistry_Models content assertions

Replace index-based want-slice with existence checks:
```go
func TestDefaultModelRegistry_Models(t *testing.T) {
    reg := provenance.DefaultModelRegistry()
    models := reg.Models()

    if len(models) == 0 {
        t.Fatal("Models() returned empty")
    }

    // Verify known models exist (not index-dependent)
    knownModels := []struct{ provider provenance.Provider; name string }{
        {provenance.ProviderAnthropic, "claude-opus-4-6"},
        {provenance.ProviderAnthropic, "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
        {provenance.ProviderAnthropic, "claude-haiku-4-5"},
    }
    for _, want := range knownModels {
        if _, ok := reg.Lookup(want.provider, want.name); !ok {
            t.Errorf("Lookup(%s, %q) should succeed", want.provider, want.name)
        }
    }
}
```

### Fix C-minor: Slice ordering clarified

Actual dependency order:
```
Slice 1 (bestiary: add APIs + tag v0.1.0) ─── must complete first
    │
Slice 0 (go get bestiary@v0.1.0) ─── needs the tag to exist
    │
Slice 2 (Provider string switch) ─── parallel with Slice 0 (no bestiary import)
    │
    ├──► Slice 3 (adapter) ─── needs Slice 0 (bestiary in go.mod) + Slice 2 (string Provider)
    │
    └──► Slice 4 (SQLite queries) ─── needs Slice 2 (string Provider)
              │
              ▼
         Slice 5 (tests) ─── needs all prior
```

Slice 1 is the true root. Slice 0 and Slice 2 can run in parallel after Slice 1.

All other slices, validation checklist, BDD acceptance criteria, and design decisions carry forward from PROPOSAL-2 (providence-ik5) without modification.

## Requirements

### R1: Provider type alignment
Provenance Provider enum switches from `type Provider int` (iota) to `type Provider string`, matching bestiary. Constants keep same names (ProviderAnthropic, etc.) with string values ("anthropic", etc.). All 44 call sites compile unchanged.

### R2: ModelEntry stays slim
ptypes.ModelEntry retains its 4 fields (Provider, Name, DisplayName, Family). ptypes package remains zero-dependency. No bestiary import in ptypes or internal/.

### R3: Adapter in root package
RegistryFromBestiary([]bestiary.ModelInfo) ModelRegistry lives in root provenance package (adapter.go). This is the only file that imports bestiary.

### R4: Default registry uses bestiary
DefaultModelRegistry() calls bestiary.StaticModels() internally via RegistryFromBestiary(). Hardcoded defaultModels slice removed. Bestiary is the single source of truth.

### R5: Bestiary API additions (cross-repo)
Add to dayvidpham/bestiary:
- LookupModelByProvider(p Provider, name string) (ModelInfo, bool)
- Models() []ModelInfo (static + synced)

### R6: SQLite schema preserved
providers table keeps integer PK with name as unique text column. Queries use subselect to resolve provider name → id. No schema migration needed for the providers table.

### R7: Bestiary version pinning
Pin bestiary dependency to a specific tag/version in go.mod, not latest main.

## Priorities
1. Provider string switch (enables everything else)
2. Bestiary API additions (prerequisite for adapter)
3. Adapter + default registry swap
4. SQLite query updates (provider name lookup)
5. Test updates

## Design Choices
- Provider is string at Go level, integer at SQLite level (bridged by providers table)
- ptypes stays zero-dependency — bestiary import isolated to root package adapter
- NewRegistry([]ModelEntry) exposed for custom registries (test, non-bestiary)

## MVP Goals
- provenance.OpenSQLite(path) uses bestiary.StaticModels() as default
- RegisterMLAgent validates via registry Lookup before DB
- All existing tests pass with string Provider

## End-Vision Goals
- provenance.OpenSQLite(path, WithModelRegistry(RegistryFromBestiary(bestiary.Client.FetchModels(ctx))))
- Live model catalog from models.dev synced into provenance
